info.title: FortiMail Engine API
info.description: Production-grade FortiMail 7.6.2 microservice. Provides authenticated, cached, and rate-limited access to FortiMail's REST API. All `/v1` routes require a Bearer API key with appropriate scopes (`admin` for `/v1/api-keys`, `fortimail:read` / `fortimail:write` for FortiMail resources).

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key from POST /v1/api-keys (requires admin scope). Format: Bearer fme_...

get.description: Returns 200 if the service process is alive. No auth required.
Returns detailed readiness data. Requires `admin` API key scope when PUBLIC_DETAILED_HEALTH is false (default in production).

post.description: Creates a new API key. Requires the `admin` scope. The raw key value (`fme_...`) is returned **once only** — it cannot be retrieved again. Store it immediately in a secure location.

key.description: Raw API key — store securely, shown ONCE

delete.description: Permanently revokes an API key. Requires the `admin` scope. Any in-flight requests using this key will be rejected immediately.

post.description: Generates a new API key for the given ID. Requires the `admin` scope. The old key remains valid for a configurable grace period (default: 5 minutes) to allow zero-downtime rotation. During the grace period, both old and new keys are accepted, but requests using the old key will log a warning.

key.description: New raw API key — store securely

oldKeyExpiresAt.description: When the old key stops being accepted

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all configured FortiMail domains. Results are cached for 5 minutes.
Returns the configuration for a specific domain by name.

post.description: Creates a new FortiMail domain with the given configuration.

put.description: Updates one or more fields of an existing domain. All fields are optional (partial update).

delete.description: Permanently deletes a domain and all its associated configuration from FortiMail.

get.description: Returns customer/account metadata for a domain (customer name, email, account limit).

put.description: Updates customer/account metadata for a domain.

get.description: Returns all mail users for the specified domain. Results are cached for 5 minutes.
Returns a specific mail user in the given domain.

post.description: Creates a new mail user in the given domain.

put.description: Updates an existing mail user (partial update — all fields optional).

delete.description: Permanently deletes a mail user from the given domain.

get.description: Returns all user mappings (internal-to-external username mappings). Cached for 5 minutes.
Returns all IMAP authentication profiles for the given domain.
Returns a specific IMAP authentication profile.

post.description: Creates a new IMAP authentication profile for the domain.

put.description: Updates an existing IMAP authentication profile.

get.description: Returns all SMTP authentication profiles for the given domain.
Returns a specific SMTP authentication profile.

post.description: Creates a new SMTP authentication profile.

put.description: Updates an existing SMTP authentication profile.

get.description: Returns all notification profiles for the given domain.
Returns a specific notification profile.

post.description: Creates a new notification profile for bounce/quarantine/delivery events.

get.description: Returns all IP geography groups used for geo-based email policies. Cached for 10 minutes.
Returns a specific IP geography group by name.

post.description: Creates a new IP geography group. Country codes must be ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g., 'US', 'CN').

put.description: Updates an existing IP geography group.

get.description: FortiMail 7.6.2: `GET /MailSetQueue` (simple list) or `GET /MailSetQueue/` (search) with encoded `extraParam`. Queue data is live (not cached).

delete.description: FortiMail 7.6.2: `DELETE /MailSetQueue` with query `mmkey` (required) and optional `extraParam` queue type. Requires `confirm: true` and header `X-Confirm-Destructive: true`.

parameters.name: x-confirm-destructive
parameters.description: Required safety header for destructive queue operations

get.description: FortiMail 7.6.2: `GET /WMMessagesRequest` with `uidScope` (mail key), `account_type`, `open_method`.

parameters.name: mkey
parameters.description: Message key from queue list (maps to uidScope)

mmkey.description: Comma-separated message keys to reroute, or 'all'

host.description: Alternate SMTP host (FortiMail `alternate`; non-25 port becomes host:port)

get.description: Returns the global SMTP proxy configuration. Cached for 5 minutes.

proxy_original.description: Whether to proxy the original SMTP connection

put.description: Updates the global SMTP proxy configuration.

proxy_original.description: Enable or disable SMTP proxy original mode

get.description: Returns available log files of the specified type. Cached for 5 minutes. Types match FortiMail log files: `alog` (history/statistics), `elog` (mail event), `vlog`, `slog`, `nlog`, `klog`. The value `mlog` is accepted for compatibility and is sent to FortiMail as `elog` (there is no `mlog` code on the appliance).

post.description: Streams a log file download directly from FortiMail. The response is streamed — not buffered in memory. Use `compressed: true` to receive a gzip-compressed file.

mmkey.description: Log file name from the list endpoint

post.description: FortiMail 7.6.2: `POST /ReportDomain_mailstats` with `{ reqAction: 7 }` only. Triggers domain-level mail statistics report generation.

get.description: Returns available generated report files. Cached for 15 minutes. Filter by `reportClass` or `domain`.
